decision.archive_summary: The thread stayed on the same Codex minor GC task and converged on a generalized fix. Earlier work added harder Chinese multitopic return cases to fixed live regressions, then tightened the packaged guard so it cannot override a shadow-guard veto. The user also set an enduring preference against whitelist-like or hardcoded handling. After that, the assistant reran the relevant live evals and reported green. The current live state is captured by the latest exchange: the user asked to continue, and the assistant said the suite is fully green after two general changes: under `continue`, any turn demoted to pin-only now blocks activation; and `summary-first` is no longer forced when raw-keep is lighter.
decision.reasoning_summary: Use `continue` because the active topic is unchanged. Evict the older solved setup/test exchanges since the latest assistant message already compresses the current technical state. Keep the durable user preference from `t5` as a semantic pin because it can still constrain future work, even if the raw turn leaves the prompt. Do not evict the latest user turn `t11`; keep the most recent assistant result `t12` as raw context for the next turn.
